drill: DEPLOY-1 closure procedure (post-REAL-1): re-confirm the deployed v3 contour against the existing log families and one /note → /ask round-trip (D-076 prep / DEPLOY-1 closure capture)

metadata.environment: real VPS — Debian/Ubuntu LTS, public DNS + HTTPS contour up via Caddy + ACME (DEPLOY-1.3 / D-062), installer-state at INSTALLER_CONFIG_VERSION=3 (DEPLOY-1.6 / D-065), real-backend env knobs active (STORAGE_BACKEND=postgres, EMBEDDING_BACKEND=openai, EMBEDDING_MODEL=text-embedding-3-large, EMBEDDING_DIMENSION=3072, CHAT_BACKEND=openai, CHAT_MODEL=gpt-4.1, TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET set)
metadata.notes: Live operator evidence for the DEPLOY-1 closure procedure (post-REAL-1). Per the DEPLOY-1 closure-procedure redaction rule (RUNBOOK §"DEPLOY-1 closure procedure (post-REAL-1) (D-076)"), credential-bearing values (PUBLIC_HOSTNAME, BACKUP_S3_BUCKET, BACKUP_S3_ENDPOINT, BACKUP_S3_PATH_PREFIX, BACKUP_S3_ACCESS_KEY_ID, BACKUP_S3_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN, TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET, OPENAI_API_KEY, webhook URL token) are replaced by <REDACTED> placeholders or a _redacted: true flag; structural outcomes (status strings, log-line shapes, status transitions, latency_ms numeric magnitudes, fallback_mode values) are preserved verbatim. Operator action required before commit: (1) copy this file to docs/deploy1-drill/deploy1-closure-post-real1-<YYYYMMDD>-evidence.json (UTC-dated), (2) drop the top-level "_template": true flag, (3) replace every <TO_FILL_BY_OPERATOR> placeholder with the verbatim captured observation, (4) run the redaction grep checklist from the RUNBOOK subsection.

live_probes._capture_note: Each branch records a structural log-line shape plus a verbatim captured line from the existing log family (no new logging contract introduced by D-077; lines captured as already emitted by the deployed v3 contour). Credential text redacted; numeric magnitudes (update_id, latency_ms, integer counters) preserved verbatim.

pg_backup_family.source: docker compose logs --tail=50 pg_backup pg_offbox_uploader | grep pg_backup
pg_backup_family.cycle_ok_line_verbatim: <TO_FILL_BY_OPERATOR — verbatim pg_backup.cycle.ok line; emitted by scripts/pg_backup/scheduler.sh after a clean backup+prune cycle>
pg_backup_family.offbox_begin_line_verbatim: <TO_FILL_BY_OPERATOR — verbatim pg_backup.offbox.begin line; emitted by scripts/pg_offbox_uploader/uploader.sh at the start of a sync>
pg_backup_family.offbox_ok_line_verbatim: <TO_FILL_BY_OPERATOR — verbatim pg_backup.offbox.ok line; emitted by scripts/pg_offbox_uploader/uploader.sh after a successful rclone sync>

caddy_access.line_verbatim: <TO_FILL_BY_OPERATOR — verbatim Caddy access-log line for one POST /telegram/webhook handled at the reverse-proxy contour (DEPLOY-1.3 / D-062); redact any PUBLIC_HOSTNAME / webhook URL token if present>

telegram_webhook.source: docker compose logs --tail=200 app | grep telegram.webhook
telegram_webhook.line_shape: telegram.webhook update_id=<id> route=<route> route_source=<src> confidence=<n/a|low|high> edit_seq=<int> lifecycle=<lifecycle> effective_path=<path>
telegram_webhook.line_verbatim: <TO_FILL_BY_OPERATOR — verbatim telegram.webhook line emitted by src/memory_rag/adapters/telegram/webhook.py; preserve update_id and route fields verbatim>

retrieval_hybrid.source: docker compose logs --tail=200 app | grep retrieval.hybrid
retrieval_hybrid.line_shape: <TO_FILL_BY_OPERATOR — record the verbatim structural shape of the retrieval.hybrid log line as currently emitted by src/memory_rag/services/retrieval.py; do not invent fields>
retrieval_hybrid.line_verbatim: <TO_FILL_BY_OPERATOR — verbatim retrieval.hybrid line from the /ask call in post_real1_round_trip below>

answer_path.line_shape: <TO_FILL_BY_OPERATOR — record the verbatim structural shape of the answer.* log line family as currently emitted by src/memory_rag/services/query_service.py and src/memory_rag/services/dispatcher.py; do not invent fields>
answer_path.line_verbatim: <TO_FILL_BY_OPERATOR — one verbatim answer.* line from the /ask call in post_real1_round_trip below>

post_real1_round_trip._capture_note: Structurally identical envelope to docs/real-answer-drill/real-answer-smoke-20260528-evidence.json (REAL-1.1 / D-074) so the closure evidence is directly comparable to REAL-1 closure evidence. Single /note → single /ask round-trip against the deployed v3 contour, fresh community recommended to avoid cross-contamination.

response_shape.body_text_verbatim: <TO_FILL_BY_OPERATOR — verbatim user-facing reply, e.g. "Saved 1 event for 2026-05-28.">

response_shape.user_facing_reply_verbatim: <TO_FILL_BY_OPERATOR — verbatim grounded user-facing reply text; should reference the saved /note content>

answer_traces_row_shape.answer_text_verbatim: <TO_FILL_BY_OPERATOR — verbatim AnswerTrace.answer_text; should match user-facing reply modulo any dispatcher trailer>

provider_attempt_line_shape.embedding_line_verbatim: <TO_FILL_BY_OPERATOR — verbatim line from grep 'provider.attempt' on app logs for the embedding call; attempt=2/3 outcome=success is acceptable if a transient 429 occurred>
provider_attempt_line_shape.chat_line_verbatim: <TO_FILL_BY_OPERATOR — verbatim line from grep 'provider.attempt' on app logs for the chat call; attempt=2/3 outcome=success is acceptable if a transient 429 occurred>

summary.closure_signals_observed: <TO_FILL_BY_OPERATOR — expected: true if installer_state shows installer_config_version=3 + all four .installer-state.json probe fields ok and live_probes captured a verbatim line from each of the five families>
summary.post_real1_round_trip_green: <TO_FILL_BY_OPERATOR — expected: true if POST /telegram/webhook for /note returned 200, POST /telegram/webhook for /ask returned 200, and answer_traces row carried fallback_mode='none', model_name='gpt-4.1', non-empty context_chunk_ids, positive latency_ms, non-empty token_counts>
summary.a43_logs_first_surface_emitting: <TO_FILL_BY_OPERATOR — expected: true if every line in live_probes.{pg_backup_family,caddy_access,telegram_webhook,retrieval_hybrid,answer_path} was captured verbatim from the deployed v3 contour with no new logging contract introduced>
summary.closes_deploy_1: <TO_FILL_BY_OPERATOR — expected: true if the three booleans above are all true>
summary.verdict: <TO_FILL_BY_OPERATOR — e.g. "DEPLOY-1 closed against the deployed v3 contour">

out_of_scope_for_this_packet: The DEPLOY-1.7b v2 → v3 cross-version upgrade drill — re-scoped to DEPLOY-2 prep by D-076; the committed template at docs/deploy1-drill/deploy1-cross-version-drill-TEMPLATE.json and the RUNBOOK subsection "v2 → v3 cross-version upgrade drill (DEPLOY-1.7b / D-068)" are retained verbatim for DEPLOY-2 use.
DEPLOY-2 managed-cloud reference deployment — deferred; A-41 stays open and is resolved when DEPLOY-2 is pulled.
Any new logging contract in src/ — A-43 closed (D-077) on the existing pg_backup.* / Caddy access / telegram.webhook / retrieval.hybrid / answer.* families with no new src/ logging.
The forward seam to remote sinks named by D-060 / D-077 — deliberately deferred to a later observability packet or DEPLOY-2 prep.
Multi-run / multi-query / quarterly closure re-capture — single-artifact closure pin per the DEPLOY-1.7a / D-067 precedent.
Any src/, schema, migration, scripts/, docker-compose.yml, Dockerfile, Caddyfile, Makefile, tests/, pyproject.toml, uv.lock, .env.example, or .gitignore change.
Any live OpenAI call inside make check or any new gated test.
Any make deploy-closure-* / make d1-close-* convenience target.
RouteKind.ENTRY → NOTE / Note / D-026 rename work.
Slice 3.7 dual-config tsvector and the unresolved D-038 Postgres baseline capture.
